headline_question: How should the broad DI-loans-n.e.c. bucket be interpreted inside the redesigned strict loan lane?

headline_direct_core.definition: Conservative same-scope direct core: mortgages plus consumer credit only.
headline_direct_core.why: Best current same-scope direct-count candidate after removing the unresolved DI-loans-n.e.c. bucket from the headline core.

standard_secondary_comparison.definition: Headline direct core plus the private-domestic borrower slice of DI loans n.e.c.
standard_secondary_comparison.why: Useful bounded comparison for how much private-borrower DI activity changes the headline read without promoting the full DI bucket into the core.

broad_loan_subtotal_diagnostic.definition: Historical broad loan subtotal including DI loans n.e.c. and other advances.
broad_loan_subtotal_diagnostic.why: Still informative as a subtotal, but no longer suitable as the headline direct core because it embeds the unresolved DI-loans-n.e.c. classification problem.

di_bucket_diagnostic.definition: Broad DI-loans-n.e.c. bucket prior to a settled core-vs-noncore split.
di_bucket_diagnostic.why: Should be interpreted through borrower and noncore/system diagnostics, not as a headline-capable direct core component.

noncore_system_diagnostic.definition: State/local, domestic-financial, ROW, borrower-gap, and other-advances subtotal.
noncore_system_diagnostic.why: Tracks the system/noncore portion of the old broad loan subtotal and helps keep the redesigned headline core narrow.

takeaways: This surface fixes the published role of the broad DI-loans-n.e.c. bucket inside the redesigned strict loan lane.
It is not a same-scope decomposition: the headline and secondary rows are strict source-side loan objects, while the borrower diagnostics remain systemwide F.215 counterpart rows.

At h0 under the core-deposit-proximate shock, the role bridge is now explicit: core residual ≈ -5.50, headline direct core ≈ -10.66, standard secondary comparison ≈ 8.49, broad loan subtotal ≈ 5.58, noncore/system diagnostic ≈ 4.32.

The private-borrower increment relative to the headline direct core is about 19.14 at h0, which is why `strict_loan_core_plus_private_borrower_qoq` is the bounded secondary comparison rather than the headline.

The broad DI bucket should remain diagnostic because the borrower-side bridge is cross-scope: the U.S.-chartered DI-loans-n.e.c. asset response is about -0.19 of the signed systemwide borrower-liability total at h0, and the dominant borrower counterpart is `strict_di_loans_nec_nonfinancial_corporate_qoq`.

The remaining named-borrower gap is about -0.00 of the signed systemwide borrower-liability total at h0, which is another reason not to publish the broad DI bucket as if it were already decomposed cleanly.
